docs.title: Baseline (Level 1)
docs.text:
Requirement Configuration User segmentation Define user segments for barrier enforcement Basic barriers Block communication between defined segments Documentation Document barrier policies and segment assignments

Minimum requirements:

  • Define user segments aligned with compliance walls
  • Configure basic Information Barrier policies
  • Document segment assignments and policy rationale
  • Manual review of agent access patterns quarterly

docs.title: Recommended (Level 2-3)
docs.text:
Requirement Configuration Agent-aware barriers Extend barriers to Copilot and agent interactions SharePoint integration Barrier enforcement on knowledge sources Automated monitoring Alert on potential barrier violations Wall-crossing workflow Documented exception process

FSI recommendations:

  • Configure Information Barriers for all Copilot interactions
  • Ensure SharePoint sites respect barrier policies
  • Implement automated alerting for barrier-related events
  • Establish formal wall-crossing approval workflow
  • Monthly review of barrier effectiveness

docs.title: Regulated/High-Risk (Level 4)
docs.text:
Requirement Configuration Comprehensive barriers All data sources and agents enforce barriers Real-time monitoring Continuous barrier enforcement verification Wall-crossing audit Full audit trail of all exceptions Third-party validation Annual review of barrier effectiveness

FSI considerations (high-risk):

  • Information Barriers mandatory for all Zone 3 agents
  • Real-time alerting on barrier events
  • Preserve barrier audit logs for 6+ years
  • Annual third-party assessment of barrier controls
  • Immediate escalation for barrier violations

docs.title: Step 1: Define User Segments
docs.text:
Portal Path: Microsoft Purview Compliance Portal → Information Barriers → Segments

  1. Sign in to Microsoft Purview Compliance Portal
  2. Navigate to Information Barriers → Segments
  3. Click New segment
  4. Configure segment using Azure AD attributes:

Example Segments for Investment Bank:

Segment Name Definition Description IB-Research Department equals "Research" Equity research analysts IB-Trading Department equals "Trading" Trading desk personnel IB-InvestmentBanking Department equals "Investment Banking" Deal team members (private side) IB-Sales Department equals "Sales" Institutional sales IB-Compliance Department equals "Compliance" Compliance and legal

PowerShell Segment Creation:

# Connect to Information Protection
Connect-IPPSSession -UserPrincipalName admin@contoso.com

# Create segments
New-OrganizationSegment -Name "IB-Research" -UserGroupFilter "Department -eq 'Research'"
New-OrganizationSegment -Name "IB-Trading" -UserGroupFilter "Department -eq 'Trading'"
New-OrganizationSegment -Name "IB-InvestmentBanking" -UserGroupFilter "Department -eq 'Investment Banking'"
New-OrganizationSegment -Name "IB-Sales" -UserGroupFilter "Department -eq 'Sales'"
New-OrganizationSegment -Name "IB-Compliance" -UserGroupFilter "Department -eq 'Compliance'"

# Verify segments
Get-OrganizationSegment | Select-Object Name, UserGroupFilter

docs.title: Step 2: Configure Information Barrier Policies
docs.text:
Portal Path: Microsoft Purview Compliance Portal → Information Barriers → Policies

  1. Navigate to Information Barriers → Policies
  2. Click Create policy
  3. Configure barrier rules:

Example Barrier Policies:

Policy Name Segment 1 Segment 2 Action Research-Trading Barrier IB-Research IB-Trading Block IB-Sales Barrier IB-InvestmentBanking IB-Sales Block Research-IB Barrier IB-Research IB-InvestmentBanking Block

PowerShell Policy Creation:

# Create barrier policies
New-InformationBarrierPolicy -Name "Research-Trading-Barrier" `
    -AssignedSegment "IB-Research" `
    -SegmentsBlocked "IB-Trading" `
    -State Active

New-InformationBarrierPolicy -Name "IB-Sales-Barrier" `
    -AssignedSegment "IB-InvestmentBanking" `
    -SegmentsBlocked "IB-Sales" `
    -State Active

New-InformationBarrierPolicy -Name "Research-IB-Barrier" `
    -AssignedSegment "IB-Research" `
    -SegmentsBlocked "IB-InvestmentBanking" `
    -State Active

# Apply policies
Start-InformationBarrierPoliciesApplication

# Check application status
Get-InformationBarrierPoliciesApplicationStatus

docs.title: Step 3: Configure Copilot and Agent Barrier Enforcement
docs.text:
Ensuring Agents Respect Information Barriers:

Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot Studio agents inherit the information barrier policies of the user invoking them. To ensure proper enforcement:

  1. Verify User Segment Assignment:
  2. Confirm all users are assigned to appropriate segments

  3. Users not in segments may bypass barriers

  4. Configure SharePoint Site Barriers:

  5. SharePoint sites used as agent knowledge sources must have barrier-compatible permissions

  6. Sites should be accessible only to appropriate segments

  7. Agent Knowledge Source Review:

  8. Audit agent knowledge sources for barrier compliance
  9. Ensure agents cannot access cross-barrier content

Agent Barrier Audit Query:

// Audit agent access across barrier boundaries
CopilotInteraction
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| extend UserSegment = tostring(parse_json(UserContext).Segment)
| extend DataSourceSegment = tostring(parse_json(DataContext).SourceSegment)
| where UserSegment != DataSourceSegment
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, UserSegment, AgentId, DataSourceSegment, Action
| summarize CrossBarrierAttempts = count() by UserSegment, DataSourceSegment, AgentId
| where CrossBarrierAttempts > 0
| order by CrossBarrierAttempts desc

docs.title: Step 4: Configure Wall-Crossing Procedures
docs.text:
Wall-Crossing Workflow:

When legitimate business needs require temporary barrier exceptions:

Wall-Crossing Approval Workflow
================================

Trigger: Wall-crossing request submitted

Workflow:
├── Capture request details
│   ├── Requestor and segment
│   ├── Target segment/person
│   ├── Business justification
│   └── Duration requested
├── Route for approval
│   ├── Compliance Officer review (mandatory)
│   ├── Legal review (if material non-public information)
│   └── Business unit head (requesting side)
├── If approved:
│   ├── Document approval with timestamp
│   ├── Add user to restricted trading list (if applicable)
│   ├── Enable temporary access
│   ├── Set automatic expiration
│   └── Notify monitoring team
├── If rejected:
│   ├── Document rejection reason
│   └── Return to requestor
├── On expiration/completion:
│   ├── Remove wall-crossing access
│   ├── Verify restricted trading list status
│   └── Archive documentation
└── Retain records for 6+ years

Wall-Crossing Documentation Template:

Field Type Description Request ID Auto-number WC-YYYY-### Requestor Person Who is crossing the wall Source Segment Choice Their home segment Target Segment Choice Segment they need access to Business Justification Multi-line Why crossing is needed Start Date Date/Time When crossing begins End Date Date/Time When crossing ends Approved By Person Compliance approver Restrictions Multi-line Trading restrictions applied Status Choice Active, Completed, Expired

docs.title: Step 5: Configure Barrier Monitoring and Alerting
docs.text:
Barrier Violation Detection:

// Detect potential barrier violations in agent interactions
let barrier_pairs = dynamic([
    {"source": "Research", "target": "Trading"},
    {"source": "InvestmentBanking", "target": "Sales"},
    {"source": "Research", "target": "InvestmentBanking"}
]);

CopilotInteraction
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| extend UserDepartment = tostring(parse_json(UserContext).Department)
| extend ContentSource = tostring(parse_json(DataContext).SourceDepartment)
| where UserDepartment != ContentSource
| extend IsCrossBarrier = iff(
    (UserDepartment == "Research" and ContentSource == "Trading") or
    (UserDepartment == "Trading" and ContentSource == "Research") or
    (UserDepartment == "InvestmentBanking" and ContentSource == "Sales") or
    (UserDepartment == "Sales" and ContentSource == "InvestmentBanking"),
    true, false)
| where IsCrossBarrier == true
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, UserDepartment, AgentId, ContentSource, UserInput
| order by TimeGenerated desc

Alert Configuration:

Alert Condition Action Barrier Violation Attempt Cross-barrier data access detected Alert Compliance immediately Unusual Cross-Segment Query Agent query references multiple segments Review within 24h Wall-Crossing Expiration Active wall-crossing approaching end Notify requestor 24h before Segment Assignment Gap User without segment assignment Alert IT for remediation

docs.title: Step 6: Zone-Specific Configuration
docs.text:
Zone 1 (Personal Productivity):

Configuration:
- Information Barriers: Inherit user's segment
- Agent Access: Personal data only
- Monitoring: Standard logging
Rationale: Personal agents have limited scope; user barriers apply automatically

Zone 2 (Team Collaboration):

Configuration:
- Information Barriers: Enforce team segment boundaries
- Agent Access: Team SharePoint only (barrier-compliant)
- Monitoring: Weekly barrier compliance review
- Alert: On cross-team access attempts
Rationale: Team agents may access shared resources; barrier enforcement critical

Zone 3 (Enterprise Managed):

Configuration:
- Information Barriers: MANDATORY enforcement
- Agent Access: Barrier-validated sources only
- Monitoring: Real-time barrier enforcement
- Alert: Immediate on any barrier event
- Wall-Crossing: Formal approval required
- Audit: Full evidence chain preserved
Rationale: Maximum protection for customer-facing and regulated agents

docs.title: Comprehensive Barrier Audit Script
docs.text:
# ============================================================
# Control 1.22: Information Barriers for AI Agents
# Barrier Compliance Audit Script
# ============================================================

param(
    [string]$OutputPath = ".\IB-Audit-$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd')"
)

New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $OutputPath -Force | Out-Null

Write-Host "=== Information Barriers Audit ===" -ForegroundColor Cyan

# ============================================================
# SECTION 1: Segment Inventory
# ============================================================

Write-Host "`nSection 1: Retrieving segments..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

Connect-IPPSSession -UserPrincipalName admin@contoso.com

$segments = Get-OrganizationSegment
$segments | Select-Object Name, UserGroupFilter, CreatedDate |
    Export-Csv "$OutputPath\Segments.csv" -NoTypeInformation

Write-Host "Found $($segments.Count) segments" -ForegroundColor Green

# ============================================================
# SECTION 2: Policy Inventory
# ============================================================

Write-Host "`nSection 2: Retrieving policies..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

$policies = Get-InformationBarrierPolicy
$policies | Select-Object Name, AssignedSegment, SegmentsBlocked, State |
    Export-Csv "$OutputPath\Policies.csv" -NoTypeInformation

Write-Host "Found $($policies.Count) policies" -ForegroundColor Green

# ============================================================
# SECTION 3: User Segment Coverage
# ============================================================

Write-Host "`nSection 3: Checking user segment coverage..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

$allUsers = Get-User -ResultSize Unlimited
$usersWithSegment = 0
$usersWithoutSegment = @()

foreach ($user in $allUsers) {
    $recipientInfo = Get-Recipient $user.UserPrincipalName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if ($recipientInfo.InformationBarrierSegment) {
        $usersWithSegment++
    } else {
        $usersWithoutSegment += $user.UserPrincipalName
    }
}

Write-Host "Users with segment: $usersWithSegment" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "Users without segment: $($usersWithoutSegment.Count)" -ForegroundColor $(if ($usersWithoutSegment.Count -gt 0) { "Yellow" } else { "Green" })

if ($usersWithoutSegment.Count -gt 0) {
    $usersWithoutSegment | Out-File "$OutputPath\Users-Without-Segment.txt"
    Write-Host "WARNING: Users without segments may bypass barriers" -ForegroundColor Yellow
}

# ============================================================
# SECTION 4: Policy Application Status
# ============================================================

Write-Host "`nSection 4: Checking policy application status..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

$appStatus = Get-InformationBarrierPoliciesApplicationStatus
Write-Host "Last application: $($appStatus.ApplicationDate)" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "Status: $($appStatus.Status)" -ForegroundColor $(if ($appStatus.Status -eq "Completed") { "Green" } else { "Yellow" })

# ============================================================
# SECTION 5: Generate Compliance Report
# ============================================================

Write-Host "`nSection 5: Generating compliance report..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

$html = @"
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Information Barriers Audit Report</title>
    <style>
        body { font-family: 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; margin: 40px; }
        h1 { color: #0078d4; }
        h2 { color: #333; border-bottom: 2px solid #0078d4; padding-bottom: 5px; }
        .dashboard { display: flex; gap: 20px; flex-wrap: wrap; margin: 20px 0; }
        .card { padding: 20px; background: #f5f5f5; border-radius: 8px; min-width: 150px; }
        .card.warning { background: #fff3cd; border-left: 4px solid #ffc107; }
        .card.success { background: #d4edda; border-left: 4px solid #28a745; }
        table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin: 20px 0; }
        th, td { padding: 10px; text-align: left; border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd; }
        th { background: #0078d4; color: white; }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>Information Barriers Audit Report</h1>
    <p>Control 1.22 | Generated: $(Get-Date -Format "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")</p>

    <h2>Summary</h2>
    <div class="dashboard">
        <div class="card"><h3>Segments</h3><p style="font-size:28px;">$($segments.Count)</p></div>
        <div class="card"><h3>Policies</h3><p style="font-size:28px;">$($policies.Count)</p></div>
        <div class="card success"><h3>Users Covered</h3><p style="font-size:28px;">$usersWithSegment</p></div>
        <div class="card $(if ($usersWithoutSegment.Count -gt 0) { 'warning' })"><h3>Users Uncovered</h3><p style="font-size:28px;">$($usersWithoutSegment.Count)</p></div>
    </div>

    <h2>Segments</h2>
    <table>
        <tr><th>Segment Name</th><th>Filter</th></tr>
        $($segments | ForEach-Object { "<tr><td>$($_.Name)</td><td>$($_.UserGroupFilter)</td></tr>" })
    </table>

    <h2>Policies</h2>
    <table>
        <tr><th>Policy Name</th><th>Assigned Segment</th><th>Blocked Segments</th><th>State</th></tr>
        $($policies | ForEach-Object { "<tr><td>$($_.Name)</td><td>$($_.AssignedSegment)</td><td>$($_.SegmentsBlocked -join ', ')</td><td>$($_.State)</td></tr>" })
    </table>

    <h2>Recommendations</h2>
    <ul>
        $(if ($usersWithoutSegment.Count -gt 0) { "<li><strong>CRITICAL:</strong> Assign segments to $($usersWithoutSegment.Count) users without segment assignment</li>" })
        <li>Verify agent knowledge sources respect barrier boundaries</li>
        <li>Review wall-crossing log for expired exceptions</li>
        <li>Schedule quarterly barrier effectiveness review</li>
    </ul>
</body>
</html>
"@

$html | Out-File -FilePath "$OutputPath\IB-Audit-Report.html" -Encoding UTF8
Write-Host "Report generated: $OutputPath\IB-Audit-Report.html" -ForegroundColor Green

Write-Host "`n=== Information Barriers Audit Complete ===" -ForegroundColor Cyan

docs.title: Regulatory Alignment
docs.text: Regulation Requirement How This Control Helps FINRA Rule 3110 Supervision of research and trading separation Enforces barriers between segments SEC Regulation SHO Short sale restrictions and information separation Prevents trading on research info FINRA Rule 5280 Trading ahead of research reports Blocks research-trading communication OCC 2011-12 Model risk management and data segregation Ensures AI respects data boundaries SEC Reg AC Analyst certification and independence Maintains research independence

docs.title: Zone-Specific Configuration
docs.text:
Zone 1 (Personal Productivity):

  • Information barriers inherited from user automatically
  • No additional configuration required
  • Personal agents cannot access cross-barrier content
  • Rationale: User context provides barrier enforcement

Zone 2 (Team Collaboration):

  • Verify team SharePoint sites respect barrier policies
  • Configure agent knowledge sources within barrier boundaries
  • Weekly audit of team agent access patterns
  • Rationale: Team collaboration must stay within barrier walls

Zone 3 (Enterprise Managed):

  • Mandatory barrier enforcement for all enterprise agents
  • Knowledge sources validated against barrier policies before deployment
  • Real-time monitoring of all data access
  • Wall-crossing requires formal approval workflow
  • Full audit trail preserved for regulatory examination
  • Rationale: Maximum protection for customer-facing and regulated functions

docs.title: FSI Implementation Example
docs.text:
Scenario: Investment Bank with Research, Trading, and Investment Banking

Barrier Configuration:

Function Segment Blocked From Equity Research IB-Research IB-Trading, IB-InvestmentBanking Trading Desk IB-Trading IB-Research, IB-InvestmentBanking Investment Banking IB-InvestmentBanking IB-Research, IB-Sales Institutional Sales IB-Sales IB-InvestmentBanking Compliance IB-Compliance None (oversight role)

Agent Restrictions:

  • Research analyst agents: Access research SharePoint only
  • Trading desk agents: Access trading systems only, no research content
  • IB deal team agents: Access deal room only, no public side data
  • Sales agents: Access client materials only, no private side data

docs.title: Verification & Testing
docs.text: Step Action Expected Result 1 Verify segments defined for all relevant business units Segments visible in Purview 2 Confirm barrier policies active between segments Policy status shows "Active" 3 Test cross-barrier query from user in Research segment Query blocked or returns no cross-barrier data 4 Test agent access respects user segment Agent cannot access cross-barrier content 5 Verify wall-crossing workflow functions Exception request routes to Compliance 6 Review barrier audit logs Barrier events logged

docs.title: Compliance Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] User segments defined for all business units requiring separation
  • [ ] Information Barrier policies configured and active
  • [ ] All users assigned to appropriate segments
  • [ ] Agent knowledge sources verified barrier-compliant
  • [ ] Wall-crossing workflow established and documented
  • [ ] Barrier monitoring and alerting configured
  • [ ] Quarterly barrier effectiveness review scheduled
  • [ ] Audit logs retained per regulatory requirements

docs.title: Issue: Users Not Assigned to Segments
docs.text:
Symptoms: Users can access cross-barrier content

Resolution:

  1. Identify users without segment assignment (run audit script)
  2. Update Azure AD attributes to match segment filters
  3. Re-run segment processing: Start-InformationBarrierPoliciesApplication
  4. Verify user segment assignment: Get-Recipient user@contoso.com | Select InformationBarrierSegment
  5. Document gap and remediation for compliance

docs.title: Issue: Agent Accessing Cross-Barrier Content
docs.text:
Symptoms: Agent returns data from blocked segments

Resolution:

  1. Verify user invoking agent has correct segment assignment
  2. Check agent knowledge source permissions
  3. Confirm SharePoint sites have barrier-compatible permissions
  4. Review agent data connections for cross-barrier sources
  5. Remove non-compliant knowledge sources from agent

docs.title: Issue: Barrier Policies Not Applying
docs.text:
Symptoms: Policies show "Active" but not enforced

Resolution:

  1. Check policy application status: Get-InformationBarrierPoliciesApplicationStatus
  2. If not "Completed", re-run: Start-InformationBarrierPoliciesApplication
  3. Wait for processing (can take several hours)
  4. Verify segment membership is correctly calculated
  5. Escalate to Microsoft support if issue persists

docs.title: Issue: Wall-Crossing Access Not Expiring
docs.text:
Symptoms: Temporary access continues after approved period

Resolution:

  1. Verify wall-crossing end date in workflow system
  2. Check automated expiration job is running
  3. Manually remove access if expired
  4. Update restricted trading list status
  5. Document incident and implement process improvement

docs.text:

  • Information Barriers in SharePoint

  • SEC Regulation AC - Analyst Certification

docs.text:
For implementation support or questions about this control, contact:

  • Purview Compliance Admin: Information Barriers configuration
  • Compliance Officer: Wall-crossing procedures and policy decisions
  • Security Administrator: Barrier monitoring and alerting
  • AI Governance Lead: Agent barrier compliance

docs.title: Control 1.23: Step-Up Authentication for AI Agent Operations
Overview
docs.text: Control ID: 1.23 Control Name: Step-Up Authentication for AI Agent Operations Regulatory Reference: GLBA 501(b), FINRA 4511, SOX 302/404, NIST SP 800-63B, Zero Trust Architecture Setup Time: 3-5 hours

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Implement step-up authentication requirements when AI agents attempt sensitive operations such as financial transactions, data exports, or access to restricted information. While Control 1.11 establishes baseline Conditional Access policies for agent authentication, this control defines operational workflows for triggering re-authentication when agents perform high-risk actions through plugins, connectors, or API calls.

Critical for Financial Transactions

Agents that can initiate or influence financial transactions, transfer customer data, or access confidential information must implement step-up authentication to verify the user's continued presence and authorization at the moment of action.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Step-Up Authentication for AI Agent Operations establishes controls to:

  • Define High-Risk Agent Actions: Catalog actions that require re-authentication before execution
  • Configure Authentication Contexts: Map sensitive actions to Entra ID Authentication Contexts
  • Implement Just-in-Time Verification: Require fresh authentication for critical operations
  • Enforce Phishing-Resistant Methods: Mandate strong authentication for high-risk scenarios
  • Audit Step-Up Events: Log all step-up authentication requests and outcomes

See Microsoft Entra Authentication Contexts for platform capabilities.

docs.title: Key Capabilities
docs.text: Capability Description FSI Relevance Authentication Context Triggers Define contexts for sensitive agent actions Granular access control Fresh Authentication Requirement Force re-auth for high-risk operations Prevents session hijacking Phishing-Resistant MFA Require FIDO2/Windows Hello for critical actions Strong authentication Connector-Level Enforcement Step-up at plugin/connector invocation Transaction security Time-Bound Sessions Limit session validity for sensitive operations Continuous verification

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Primary Owner Admin Role: Security Administrator Supporting Roles: Power Platform Admin, Entra ID Admin, Compliance Officer

docs.title: Licenses Required
docs.text: License Purpose Required For Microsoft Entra ID P1 Conditional Access All levels Microsoft Entra ID P2 Authentication Contexts, Risk-based CA Level 2+ Power Platform per-user Agent governance All levels Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps Session control Level 4

docs.title: Permissions Required
docs.text: Role Purpose Assignment Method Conditional Access Administrator Create/manage CA policies Entra ID Security Administrator Authentication context management Entra ID Power Platform Admin Connector policies Power Platform Admin Center Application Administrator Service principal configuration Entra ID

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: Dependency Description Verification Control 1.11 (Conditional Access) Baseline CA policies Verify CA configured Control 1.4 (Advanced Connector Policies) Connector governance Check ACP setup Control 1.18 (RBAC) Role-based access Verify roles defined Control 1.7 (Audit Logging) Authentication logging Check Purview Audit

docs.title: Pre-Setup Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Microsoft Entra ID P2 licensing confirmed
  • [ ] Baseline Conditional Access policies configured (Control 1.11)
  • [ ] High-risk agent actions inventoried
  • [ ] Authentication Contexts feature enabled
  • [ ] Phishing-resistant MFA methods deployed (FIDO2, Windows Hello)
  • [ ] Plugin/connector inventory with risk classification

docs.title: Baseline (Level 1)
docs.text:
Requirement Configuration MFA enforcement All agent interactions require MFA Session limits 8-hour session maximum Documentation Document high-risk actions

Minimum requirements:

  • Baseline MFA for all Copilot and agent access
  • Document actions that would benefit from step-up authentication
  • Standard session timeout policies
  • Quarterly review of authentication requirements

docs.title: Recommended (Level 2-3)
docs.text:
Requirement Configuration Authentication Contexts Define contexts for sensitive actions Step-up triggers Configure triggers for financial/data actions Fresh authentication 15-minute max age for sensitive operations Risk-based elevation Require stronger auth when risk detected

FSI recommendations:

  • Configure Authentication Contexts for high-risk agent actions
  • Implement step-up authentication for:
  • Financial transaction initiation
  • Customer data exports
  • External API calls with customer data
  • Agent configuration changes
  • Require phishing-resistant MFA for step-up scenarios
  • Monthly review of step-up authentication events

docs.title: Regulated/High-Risk (Level 4)
docs.text:
Requirement Configuration Mandatory step-up All sensitive actions require fresh auth Continuous verification Session verified before each high-risk action Hardware token requirement FIDO2/hardware key for critical operations Real-time monitoring Alert on step-up failures

FSI considerations (high-risk):

  • Step-up authentication mandatory for all Zone 3 agent operations
  • Phishing-resistant MFA required (no SMS/phone call)
  • Session maximum of 4 hours with step-up at each sensitive action
  • Real-time alerting on step-up authentication failures
  • Full audit trail preserved for regulatory examination
  • Third-party validation of step-up controls

docs.title: Step 1: Define High-Risk Agent Actions
docs.text:
Action Classification Framework:

Identify actions that require step-up authentication based on risk level:

Risk Level Action Category Examples Step-Up Requirement Critical Financial Transactions Trade execution, wire transfers, payment initiation Always required Critical Data Export Customer data download, bulk export, API extract Always required High External Integration CRM updates, third-party API calls with PII Required High Configuration Changes Agent permissions, connector settings, knowledge sources Required Medium Sensitive Queries Account balance, portfolio details, transaction history Risk-based Low General Assistance FAQ, general information, scheduling Standard MFA

Agent Action Risk Assessment Template:

# Agent Action Risk Assessment

## Agent Information
- **Agent Name/ID:** [Name]
- **Agent Zone:** [1/2/3]
- **Primary Function:** [Description]

## Actions Inventory
| Action | Description | Data Sensitivity | External System | Step-Up Required |
|--------|-------------|------------------|-----------------|------------------|
| [Action 1] | [Description] | [High/Med/Low] | [Yes/No] | [Yes/No/Risk-based] |

## Recommended Authentication Context
- **Context Name:** [e.g., "FinancialTransactions"]
- **Authentication Strength:** [Phishing-resistant/MFA/Password]
- **Max Session Age:** [Minutes]
- **Location Restrictions:** [Yes/No]

## Approval
- Assessed By: _________________ Date: _________
- Approved By: _________________ Date: _________

docs.title: Step 2: Create Authentication Contexts in Entra ID
docs.text:
Portal Path: Microsoft Entra Admin Center → Protection → Conditional Access → Authentication Contexts

  1. Navigate to Microsoft Entra Admin Center (entra.microsoft.com)
  2. Go to Protection → Conditional Access → Authentication Contexts
  3. Click + New authentication context
  4. Create contexts for agent operations:

Recommended Authentication Contexts for FSI:

Context Name ID Description Use Case c1 AgentFinancialTransaction Financial transaction operations Trade execution, transfers c2 AgentDataExport Data export operations Customer data, bulk exports c3 AgentExternalAPI External system calls CRM, third-party APIs c4 AgentConfigChange Configuration modifications Agent settings, permissions c5 AgentSensitiveQuery Sensitive data queries Account details, balances

PowerShell Configuration:

# Connect to Microsoft Graph
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes "Policy.ReadWrite.ConditionalAccess"

# Create Authentication Context for Financial Transactions
$financialContext = @{
    DisplayName = "Agent Financial Transaction"
    Description = "Step-up authentication for AI agent financial operations"
    Id = "c1"
    IsAvailable = $true
}

# Note: Authentication contexts are created through the portal or Graph API
# This shows the configuration structure
Write-Host "Create authentication contexts in Entra Admin Center:"
Write-Host "  - c1: Agent Financial Transaction"
Write-Host "  - c2: Agent Data Export"
Write-Host "  - c3: Agent External API"
Write-Host "  - c4: Agent Config Change"
Write-Host "  - c5: Agent Sensitive Query"

docs.title: Step 3: Configure Conditional Access Policies for Step-Up
docs.text:
Portal Path: Microsoft Entra Admin Center → Protection → Conditional Access → Policies

Policy 1: Step-Up for Financial Transactions

  1. Click + Create new policy
  2. Name: FSI-Agent-StepUp-FinancialTransactions
  3. Assignments:
  4. Users: All users (or specific groups with agent access)
  5. Target resources: Authentication contexts → c1 (Agent Financial Transaction)
  6. Conditions:
  7. Sign-in risk: Not configured (applies regardless of risk)
  8. Grant:
  9. Grant access
  10. Require authentication strength: Phishing-resistant MFA
  11. Require compliant device (recommended)
  12. Session:
  13. Sign-in frequency: 15 minutes (or every time)
  14. Enable policy: On

Policy 2: Step-Up for Data Export

  1. Create new policy: FSI-Agent-StepUp-DataExport
  2. Target: Authentication context c2
  3. Grant: Phishing-resistant MFA + Compliant device
  4. Session: Sign-in frequency every 30 minutes

Policy 3: Risk-Based Step-Up for Sensitive Queries

  1. Create new policy: FSI-Agent-StepUp-RiskBased
  2. Target: Authentication context c5
  3. Conditions:
  4. Sign-in risk: Medium and above
  5. User risk: Low and above
  6. Grant: MFA required
  7. Session: Sign-in frequency 1 hour

PowerShell: Create Step-Up CA Policy

# Connect to Microsoft Graph
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes "Policy.ReadWrite.ConditionalAccess"

# Define policy for financial transactions step-up
$policyParams = @{
    DisplayName = "FSI-Agent-StepUp-FinancialTransactions"
    State = "enabledForReportingButNotEnforced"  # Start in report-only
    Conditions = @{
        Applications = @{
            IncludeAuthenticationContextClassReferences = @("c1")
        }
        Users = @{
            IncludeUsers = @("All")
        }
    }
    GrantControls = @{
        Operator = "AND"
        AuthenticationStrength = @{
            Id = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000004"  # Phishing-resistant MFA
        }
        BuiltInControls = @("compliantDevice")
    }
    SessionControls = @{
        SignInFrequency = @{
            Value = 15
            Type = "minutes"
            IsEnabled = $true
            AuthenticationType = "primaryAndSecondaryAuthentication"
        }
    }
}

# Create the policy
$policy = New-MgIdentityConditionalAccessPolicy -BodyParameter $policyParams
Write-Host "Created policy: $($policy.DisplayName)" -ForegroundColor Green

# Note: Test in report-only mode before enabling
Write-Host "Policy is in report-only mode. Review sign-in logs before enabling." -ForegroundColor Yellow

docs.title: Step 4: Integrate Step-Up with Power Platform Connectors
docs.text:
Approach: Configure custom connectors and actions to require specific authentication contexts.

Custom Connector Authentication Configuration:

For connectors that perform sensitive operations, configure OAuth to require step-up:

# Custom Connector Definition (excerpt)
securityDefinitions:
  oauth2_auth:
    type: oauth2
    flow: accessCode
    authorizationUrl: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/authorize
    tokenUrl: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token
    scopes:
      User.Read: Read user profile
      FinancialOperations.Execute: Execute financial operations
    x-ms-authentication-context: c1  # Require financial transaction context

Action-Level Step-Up Configuration:

For built-in connectors, use DLP policies to block sensitive actions and route through step-up enabled alternatives:

  1. Block direct sensitive actions in DLP policy
  2. Create custom connector that wraps the action with step-up requirement
  3. Route sensitive operations through the step-up connector

docs.title: Step 5: Implement Service Principal Step-Up for Agent Workloads
docs.text:
Scenario: When agents operate as service principals (for background operations), configure workload identity risk detection and step-up equivalent controls.

Workload Identity Conditional Access (Preview):

# Configure Conditional Access for workload identities
# This applies to service principals used by agents

$workloadPolicy = @{
    DisplayName = "FSI-Agent-ServicePrincipal-HighRisk"
    State = "enabled"
    Conditions = @{
        Applications = @{
            IncludeApplications = @("ServicePrincipal")
        }
        ServicePrincipalRiskLevels = @("high", "medium")
    }
    GrantControls = @{
        Operator = "OR"
        BuiltInControls = @("block")
    }
}

# Note: Workload identity CA requires Entra ID P2
Write-Host "Configure workload identity policies in Entra Admin Center"
Write-Host "Block high-risk service principal sign-ins"

Agent Workload Step-Up Equivalent:

Since service principals cannot perform interactive MFA, implement compensating controls:

Scenario Compensating Control High-risk SP sign-in detected Block and alert; require human approval Sensitive action initiated Require approval workflow before execution Unusual behavior detected Pause operation and notify Security After-hours operation Require pre-authorization

docs.title: Step 6: Configure Session Controls for Agent Interactions
docs.text:
Portal Path: Microsoft Entra Admin Center → Protection → Conditional Access → Session

Session Control Configuration:

Control Zone 1 Zone 2 Zone 3 Sign-in frequency 8 hours 4 hours 1 hour Persistent sessions Allowed Disallowed Disallowed Step-up for sensitive actions Optional Required Required App-enforced restrictions No Yes Yes

Defender for Cloud Apps Session Control (Level 4):

For regulated environments, use Defender for Cloud Apps Conditional Access App Control:

  1. Navigate to Defender for Cloud Apps portal
  2. Go to Control → Policies → Conditional Access App Control
  3. Create policy for session monitoring:
  4. Monitor all agent-related activities
  5. Block download of sensitive content without step-up
  6. Require approval for bulk operations

docs.title: Step 7: Implement Step-Up Workflow for Agents
docs.text:
Agent Step-Up Integration Pattern:

User Invokes Agent → Agent Processes Request → Sensitive Action Detected?
                                                       │
                                   ┌───────────────────┴───────────────────┐
                                   │                                       │
                                  No                                      Yes
                                   │                                       │
                                   ▼                                       ▼
                            Execute Action                      Check Last Auth Time
                                                                       │
                                              ┌────────────────────────┴────────────────────────┐
                                              │                                                 │
                                       Fresh (<15 min)                                   Stale (>15 min)
                                              │                                                 │
                                              ▼                                                 ▼
                                       Execute Action                               Trigger Step-Up Auth
                                                                                           │
                                                                              ┌────────────┴────────────┐
                                                                              │                         │
                                                                        Auth Success              Auth Failure
                                                                              │                         │
                                                                              ▼                         ▼
                                                                       Execute Action           Block & Log

Implementation in Power Automate:

# Step-Up Check Flow (Conceptual)
trigger:
  - agent_sensitive_action

steps:
  - action: check_authentication_context
    inputs:
      required_context: "c1"  # Financial transaction context
      max_age_minutes: 15

  - condition: auth_context_valid
    if_true:
      - execute_sensitive_action
    if_false:
      - return_step_up_required
      - log_step_up_request

docs.title: Step 8: Configure Monitoring and Alerting
docs.text:
Step-Up Authentication Monitoring:

// Monitor step-up authentication events
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where AuthenticationContextClassReferences has_any ("c1", "c2", "c3", "c4", "c5")
| extend StepUpContext = tostring(AuthenticationContextClassReferences)
| extend StepUpResult = iff(ResultType == 0, "Success", "Failure")
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, AppDisplayName, StepUpContext, StepUpResult, ResultDescription
| summarize
    TotalAttempts = count(),
    Successes = countif(StepUpResult == "Success"),
    Failures = countif(StepUpResult == "Failure")
    by StepUpContext, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

Alert Configuration:

Alert Condition Action Step-Up Failure Spike >5 failures in 15 minutes Alert Security team Sensitive Action Without Step-Up Action executed without required context Alert Compliance Bypass Attempt Request for step-up waiver Alert AI Governance Lead After-Hours Sensitive Action High-risk action outside business hours Alert Security

PowerShell: Step-Up Audit Report

# Generate Step-Up Authentication Audit Report

Connect-MgGraph -Scopes "AuditLog.Read.All", "Directory.Read.All"

$startDate = (Get-Date).AddDays(-30).ToString("yyyy-MM-ddT00:00:00Z")
$endDate = (Get-Date).ToString("yyyy-MM-ddT23:59:59Z")

# Query sign-in logs with authentication context
$filter = "createdDateTime ge $startDate and createdDateTime le $endDate"
$signIns = Get-MgAuditLogSignIn -Filter $filter -All

$stepUpEvents = $signIns | Where-Object {
    $_.AuthenticationContextClassReferences -ne $null -and
    $_.AuthenticationContextClassReferences.Count -gt 0
}

$report = $stepUpEvents | ForEach-Object {
    [PSCustomObject]@{
        Timestamp = $_.CreatedDateTime
        User = $_.UserPrincipalName
        Application = $_.AppDisplayName
        AuthContext = ($_.AuthenticationContextClassReferences -join ", ")
        Result = if ($_.Status.ErrorCode -eq 0) { "Success" } else { "Failure" }
        ErrorDetail = $_.Status.FailureReason
        Location = "$($_.Location.City), $($_.Location.CountryOrRegion)"
        Device = $_.DeviceDetail.DisplayName
    }
}

$report | Export-Csv -Path ".\StepUp-Audit-$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd').csv" -NoTypeInformation
Write-Host "Exported $($report.Count) step-up authentication events" -ForegroundColor Green

# Summary statistics
$summary = $report | Group-Object Result | Select-Object Name, Count
Write-Host "`nStep-Up Summary:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
$summary | Format-Table

docs.title: Regulatory Alignment
docs.text: Regulation Requirement How This Control Helps GLBA 501(b) Customer information safeguards Verifies user identity before sensitive data access FINRA 4511 Books and records security Ensures authorized access to financial records SOX 302/404 Internal controls Provides transaction-level authentication controls NIST 800-63B Digital identity guidelines Implements AAL2/AAL3 for sensitive operations Zero Trust Continuous verification Validates identity at moment of sensitive action

docs.title: Zone-Specific Configuration
docs.text:
Zone 1 (Personal Productivity):

Configuration:
  - Step-up authentication: Not required
  - Session timeout: 8 hours
  - Standard MFA: Required
Rationale: Personal productivity agents have limited risk exposure

Zone 2 (Team Collaboration):

Configuration:
  - Step-up authentication: Required for data exports and external API calls
  - Authentication contexts: c2 (Data Export), c3 (External API)
  - Session timeout: 4 hours
  - Fresh auth for sensitive actions: 30 minutes
Rationale: Team agents may access shared data requiring additional verification

Zone 3 (Enterprise Managed):

Configuration:
  - Step-up authentication: MANDATORY for all sensitive actions
  - Authentication contexts: c1-c5 (all contexts)
  - Session timeout: 1 hour
  - Fresh auth for sensitive actions: 15 minutes
  - Phishing-resistant MFA: Required
  - Device compliance: Required
  - Real-time monitoring: Enabled
Rationale: Customer-facing and transaction-capable agents require strictest controls

docs.title: FSI Implementation Example
docs.text:
Scenario: Trading Platform Agent with Order Execution Capability

Step-Up Configuration:

agent_configuration:
  name: "Trading Assistant Agent"
  zone: 3
  risk_classification: "Critical"

  actions:
    - action: "view_portfolio"
      risk: "medium"
      auth_context: "c5"  # Sensitive query
      step_up_required: "risk_based"
      max_auth_age: "30m"

    - action: "execute_trade"
      risk: "critical"
      auth_context: "c1"  # Financial transaction
      step_up_required: "always"
      max_auth_age: "5m"
      additional_requirements:
        - "phishing_resistant_mfa"
        - "compliant_device"
        - "network_location_verified"

    - action: "export_trade_history"
      risk: "high"
      auth_context: "c2"  # Data export
      step_up_required: "always"
      max_auth_age: "15m"

  monitoring:
    step_up_failures: "alert_immediately"
    sensitive_actions: "log_all"
    after_hours_trades: "require_approval"

docs.title: Verification & Testing
docs.text: Step Action Expected Result 1 Access agent without MFA Access denied 2 Complete MFA, access agent Access granted 3 Wait >15 minutes, attempt financial action Step-up authentication triggered 4 Complete step-up with FIDO2 Action executed 5 Attempt step-up with SMS code Denied (if phishing-resistant required) 6 Review audit logs Step-up events logged

docs.title: Compliance Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] High-risk agent actions inventoried
  • [ ] Authentication contexts created in Entra ID
  • [ ] Conditional Access policies configured for each context
  • [ ] Session controls configured per zone
  • [ ] Phishing-resistant MFA required for critical actions
  • [ ] Monitoring and alerting configured
  • [ ] Step-up audit logs retained per policy
  • [ ] User training completed on step-up requirements
  • [ ] Quarterly review of step-up effectiveness scheduled

docs.title: Issue: Step-Up Not Triggering for Sensitive Actions
docs.text:
Symptoms: Users can perform sensitive actions without re-authentication

Resolution:

  1. Verify authentication context is correctly configured
  2. Check CA policy targets the correct authentication context
  3. Confirm application/connector is tagged to require the context
  4. Review sign-in logs for context claim presence
  5. Test with a new session (clear browser cache)

docs.title: Issue: Step-Up Blocking All Actions
docs.text:
Symptoms: Users cannot perform any agent operations

Resolution:

  1. Review CA policy scope (may be too broad)
  2. Check if session frequency is too aggressive
  3. Verify user has compliant device (if required)
  4. Confirm user has registered phishing-resistant MFA
  5. Test with policy in report-only mode first

docs.title: Issue: Service Principal Operations Blocked
docs.text:
Symptoms: Background agent operations failing authentication

Resolution:

  1. Service principals cannot do interactive MFA
  2. Implement approval workflow for sensitive SP operations
  3. Configure workload identity risk policies
  4. Review service principal sign-in logs
  5. Consider managed identity where possible

docs.title: Issue: Users Frustrated by Frequent Step-Ups
docs.text:
Symptoms: Complaints about too many authentication prompts

Resolution:

  1. Review action classification (may be over-classified)
  2. Extend session frequency for lower-risk actions
  3. Ensure SSO is working correctly
  4. Train users on the purpose of step-up
  5. Consider risk-based step-up instead of always-on

docs.title: Additional Resources
docs.text:
• Authentication contexts in Conditional Access
  • Authentication strengths
  • Sign-in frequency and session controls
  • Phishing-resistant MFA methods
  • NIST SP 800-63B Digital Identity Guidelines

docs.title: Related Controls
docs.text: Control Relationship Control 1.11: Conditional Access Baseline CA policies; step-up builds on top Control 1.4: Advanced Connector Policies Connector governance enables action-level step-up Control 1.18: RBAC Role-based access complements step-up Control 1.7: Audit Logging Step-up events must be logged Control 2.12: Supervision Supervision of sensitive agent actions

docs.title: Support & Questions
docs.text:
For implementation support or questions about this control, contact:

  • Security Administrator: Conditional Access and authentication context setup
  • Entra ID Admin: Authentication strength and MFA configuration
  • Power Platform Admin: Connector-level step-up implementation
  • Compliance Officer: Action classification and regulatory requirements

Updated: Jan 2026 Version: v1.0 (Jan 2026) UI Verification Status: ❌ Needs verification

docs.title: Control 1.3: SharePoint Content Governance and Permissions
Overview
docs.text: Control ID: 1.3 Control Name: SharePoint Content Governance and Permissions Regulatory Reference: GLBA 501(b), 504(b), SEC Reg S-P, FINRA 4511 Setup Time: 2-4 hours (initial configuration); ongoing management

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
SharePoint is often the primary knowledge source for Copilot Studio agents. Proper content governance ensures agents only access authorized data and prevents inadvertent exposure of sensitive customer information. This control is critical for:

  • GLBA 501(b): Safeguarding customer NPI stored in SharePoint
  • SEC Reg S-P: Protecting customer records in document libraries
  • FINRA 4511: Maintaining books and records with appropriate access controls
  • Data Minimization: Ensuring agents access only necessary content
  • Oversharing Prevention: Stopping accidental exposure through AI responses

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Primary Owner Admin Role: SharePoint Admin Supporting Roles: SharePoint Site Collection Admin

docs.title: Required Licenses
docs.text:
• SharePoint Online (included in Microsoft 365 E3/E5)
  • Microsoft 365 E5 or SharePoint Advanced Management (for enhanced controls)
  • Microsoft Purview (for sensitivity labels)

docs.title: Required Permissions
docs.text:
• SharePoint Administrator (tenant-level settings)
  • Site Collection Administrator (site-level configuration)
  • Purview Compliance Administrator (sensitivity labels)

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Control 1.5 (DLP & Sensitivity Labels): Labels must be created before applying to sites/libraries
  • Control 4.1 (IAG): Information Access Governance for restricted content discovery

docs.title: Pre-Setup Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Audit existing SharePoint sites with sensitive data
  • [ ] Identify sites that will be agent knowledge sources
  • [ ] Define sensitivity label taxonomy
  • [ ] Create site templates for FSI governance
  • [ ] Document external sharing requirements
  • [ ] Plan access review schedule

docs.title: Baseline (Level 1)
docs.text: Least-privilege SharePoint permissions; restrict external sharing; label sensitive libraries.

docs.title: Recommended (Level 2-3)
docs.text: Standardized site templates + sensitivity labels; periodic access reviews; restrict agent access to approved sites.

docs.title: Regulated/High-Risk (Level 4)
docs.text: Admin-controlled permissions with legal review; immutable retention; continuous access monitoring.

docs.title: Step 1: Configure Tenant-Level Sharing Settings
docs.text:
Portal Path: SharePoint Admin Center → Policies → Sharing

  1. Navigate to SharePoint Admin Center
  2. Go to Policies → Sharing
  3. Configure organization-level settings:

FSI Recommended Settings:

Setting Recommended Value Rationale External sharing level Only people in your organization Prevent accidental customer data exposure File and folder links Only people in your organization No anonymous links Allow or block sharing with specific domains Block consumer domains (gmail.com, yahoo.com) Prevent consumer email sharing Guests must sign in using the same account Enabled Ensure accountability Allow guests to share items they don't own Disabled Prevent chain sharing Guest access expires automatically 30-90 days Limit persistent guest access
  1. Click Save

docs.title: Step 2: Configure Site-Level Settings for Agent Knowledge Sources
docs.text:
Portal Path: SharePoint Admin Center → Sites → Active sites → [Select site]

For each SharePoint site that agents will use as a knowledge source:

  1. Select the site → Click Sharing
  2. Set sharing level:
  3. Enterprise-managed agents: Only people in your organization
  4. Team collaboration agents: New and existing guests (with approval)
  5. Personal productivity agents: Site owner discretion
  6. Click Permissions → Advanced permissions settings
  7. Review and configure:
  8. Remove Everyone and Everyone except external users groups
  9. Add specific security groups with appropriate roles
  10. Document all permission grants

Configure Site Settings in SharePoint:

  1. Navigate to the site directly
  2. Go to Site settings (gear icon → Site information → View all site settings)
  3. Under Site Administration:
  4. Site permissions: Verify minimal permission grants
  5. Site collection features: Enable required features
  6. Under Site Actions:
  7. Manage site features: Enable auditing if available

docs.title: Step 3: Apply Sensitivity Labels to Sites and Libraries
docs.text: Portal Path: Microsoft Purview → Information protection → Labels

docs.title: Apply Label at Site Level
docs.text:
1. In SharePoint Admin Center → Sites → Active sites
  2. Select the site → Policies tab
  3. Under Sensitivity, click Edit
  4. Select appropriate label:
  5. Confidential-FSI for customer data
  6. Highly Confidential for trading/material non-public information
  7. Internal for general business content
  8. Click Save

docs.title: Apply Label at Library Level
docs.text:
1. Navigate to the document library in SharePoint
  2. Click Settings (gear) → Library settings
  3. Under Permissions and Management:
  4. Click Apply sensitivity label to items in this list or library
  5. Select the appropriate default label
  6. Choose whether to:
  7. Apply label to existing items: Recommended for initial deployment
  8. Apply label only to new items: For ongoing governance

docs.title: Step 4: Create Restricted Sites for Sensitive Agent Knowledge
docs.text:
Portal Path: SharePoint Admin Center → Sites → Create

Create dedicated sites for agent knowledge sources:

  1. Click Create → Choose Team site or Communication site
  2. Configure site settings:
  3. Site name: Agent-Knowledge-[AgentName] or FSI-AgentData-[Zone]
  4. Privacy settings: Private
  5. Language: English
  6. After creation, configure:
  7. External sharing: Only people in your organization
  8. Apply sensitivity label: Appropriate FSI label
  9. Site permissions: Only agent service accounts + content owners

docs.title: Step 5: Configure Information Access Governance (IAG)
docs.text:
Portal Path: SharePoint Admin Center → Sites → Active sites → Restricted access control

For enterprise-managed agent knowledge sources:

  1. Select the site containing sensitive data
  2. Click Policies → Restricted access control
  3. Enable Restrict access to this site
  4. Add specific users or groups who can access
  5. This creates an additional layer beyond standard permissions

Configure Restricted Content Discoverability:

  1. Navigate to SharePoint Admin Center → Content services → Information access governance
  2. Create a new restricted content discoverability policy
  3. Define:
  4. Sites/content to restrict
  5. Users/groups who can discover restricted content
  6. Exceptions for agents that need access

docs.title: Step 6: Configure Access Reviews for Agent Knowledge Sites
docs.text:
Portal Path: Microsoft Entra → Identity Governance → Access Reviews

  1. Navigate to Entra Admin Center → Identity Governance → Access Reviews
  2. Click + New access review
  3. Configure:
  4. Name: Agent-Knowledge-Sites-Quarterly-Review
  5. Scope: SharePoint site(s) used by agents
  6. Reviewers: Site owners or designated governance team
  7. Frequency: Quarterly (for enterprise-managed) or Semi-annually (team collaboration)
  8. Duration: 14 days
  9. Under Upon completion settings:
  10. Auto-apply results: Consider for personal productivity / team collaboration
  11. If reviewers don't respond: Remove access (for enterprise-managed)
  12. Create the access review

docs.title: Step 7: Configure PowerShell Site Governance
docs.text:
# SharePoint Content Governance Configuration
# Requires: SharePoint Online Management Shell

# Install module if needed
Install-Module -Name Microsoft.Online.SharePoint.PowerShell -Force

# Connect to SharePoint Online
$AdminUrl = "https://contoso-admin.sharepoint.com"
Connect-SPOService -Url $AdminUrl

# Get all sites and their sharing settings
$Sites = Get-SPOSite -Limit All
$SiteReport = $Sites | Select-Object Url, Title, Owner, SharingCapability,
    ConditionalAccessPolicy, SensitivityLabel, LockState |
    Export-Csv "C:\Governance\SharePoint-Sites-$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd').csv" -NoTypeInformation

# Configure a site for an enterprise-managed agent knowledge source
$AgentKnowledgeSite = "https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/Agent-CustomerService"

Set-SPOSite -Identity $AgentKnowledgeSite `
    -SharingCapability Disabled `
    -DisableSharingForNonOwners $true `
    -DefaultLinkPermission View `
    -ConditionalAccessPolicy AllowLimitedAccess `
    -LimitedAccessFileType OfficeOnlineFilesOnly

# Apply sensitivity label to site (requires Graph API)
# Connect to Graph
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes "Sites.ReadWrite.All", "InformationProtectionPolicy.Read"

# Get the site ID
$Site = Get-MgSite -SiteId "contoso.sharepoint.com:/sites/Agent-CustomerService"

# Get available labels
$Labels = Get-MgInformationProtectionLabel
$TargetLabel = $Labels | Where-Object { $_.Name -eq "Confidential-FSI" }

# Apply label to site
# Note: Site sensitivity labels are set via site classification during creation
# or via the SharePoint Admin Center for existing sites

# Remove overly permissive groups from sites
$AgentSites = @(
    "https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/Agent-CustomerService",
    "https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/Agent-Trading",
    "https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/Agent-Compliance"
)

foreach ($SiteUrl in $AgentSites) {
    # Get site groups
    $Site = Get-SPOSite -Identity $SiteUrl -Detailed

    # Remove Everyone and Everyone except external users if present
    try {
        Remove-SPOUser -Site $SiteUrl -LoginName "c:0(.s|true"  # Everyone
        Write-Host "Removed 'Everyone' from $SiteUrl" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    } catch {
        Write-Host "'Everyone' not found on $SiteUrl" -ForegroundColor Gray
    }

    try {
        Remove-SPOUser -Site $SiteUrl -LoginName "c:0-.f|rolemanager|spo-grid-all-users/$($Site.Id)"
        Write-Host "Removed 'Everyone except external users' from $SiteUrl" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    } catch {
        Write-Host "'Everyone except external' not found on $SiteUrl" -ForegroundColor Gray
    }
}

# Generate permission report for agent knowledge sites
$PermissionReport = @()

foreach ($SiteUrl in $AgentSites) {
    $SiteUsers = Get-SPOUser -Site $SiteUrl -Limit All

    foreach ($User in $SiteUsers) {
        $PermissionReport += [PSCustomObject]@{
            Site = $SiteUrl
            LoginName = $User.LoginName
            DisplayName = $User.DisplayName
            IsSiteAdmin = $User.IsSiteAdmin
            Groups = ($User.Groups -join "; ")
        }
    }
}

$PermissionReport | Export-Csv "C:\Governance\AgentSites-Permissions-$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd').csv" -NoTypeInformation
Write-Host "Permission report exported" -ForegroundColor Green

# Configure external sharing for each site
Set-SPOSite -Identity "https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/Agent-CustomerService" `
    -SharingCapability Disabled

Set-SPOSite -Identity "https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/Agent-Trading" `
    -SharingCapability Disabled

# Enable auditing at tenant level
Set-SPOTenant -DisabledWebPartIds @() # Ensure web parts not blocking audit
# Note: Unified audit log is enabled via Security & Compliance Center

docs.title: Regulatory Alignment
docs.text: Regulation SharePoint Governance Requirement GLBA 501(b) Restrict access to sites containing customer NPI; log access SEC Reg S-P Privacy safeguards for customer records in document libraries FINRA 4511 Maintain books and records with access controls; audit trail SOX 302/404 Segregation of duties for financial document access

docs.title: Zone 1 (Personal Productivity)
docs.text:
Site Sharing: Site owner discretion
External Sharing: Organization-wide setting
Sensitivity Labels: Recommended but not required
Access Reviews: Annual
Permissions: Standard SharePoint groups

docs.title: Zone 2 (Team Collaboration)
docs.text:
Site Sharing: Existing guests only (with approval)
External Sharing: Specific partner domains only
Sensitivity Labels: Required - minimum "Internal"
Access Reviews: Semi-annual
Permissions: Named security groups only

docs.title: Zone 3 (Enterprise Managed)
docs.text:
Site Sharing: Organization only (no external)
External Sharing: Disabled completely
Sensitivity Labels: Required - "Confidential-FSI" or higher
Access Reviews: Quarterly
Permissions: Named individuals + service accounts only
Additional: IAG/Restricted Content Discoverability enabled

docs.title: FSI Configuration Example: Wealth Management Firm
docs.text:
Scenario: A wealth management firm uses Copilot Studio agents to answer client questions using portfolio documentation stored in SharePoint.

SharePoint Structure:

/sites/ClientPortfolios-WM/
├── Client-Onboarding/          [Sensitivity: Highly Confidential]
│   ├── KYC Documents/
│   ├── Account Opening Forms/
│   └── Risk Assessments/
├── Investment-Research/        [Sensitivity: Confidential-FSI]
│   ├── Analyst Reports/
│   ├── Market Data/
│   └── Model Portfolios/
├── Agent-Knowledge-Base/       [Sensitivity: Confidential-FSI]
│   ├── Product Information/
│   ├── Fee Schedules/
│   ├── FAQ Content/
│   └── Approved Disclosures/
└── Compliance-Archive/         [Sensitivity: Highly Confidential - Regulatory]
    ├── Audit Reports/
    ├── Regulatory Correspondence/
    └── Examination Materials/

Agent Configuration:

  1. Client Inquiry Agent (enterprise-managed):
  2. Access: /Agent-Knowledge-Base/ only
  3. Permissions: Read-only via service account
  4. Sensitivity: Can only return Confidential or lower content

  5. Excluded: Client-Onboarding (PII), Compliance-Archive

  6. Internal Research Agent (team collaboration):

  7. Access: /Investment-Research/ + /Agent-Knowledge-Base/
  8. Permissions: Read via analyst security group
  9. Sensitivity: Can access Confidential-FSI content

Governance Measures:

  • Weekly access review for Client Inquiry Agent service account
  • Audit log monitoring for unusual access patterns
  • Quarterly recertification of agent data source permissions
  • DLP policies prevent agent from exposing SSN, account numbers in responses

docs.title: Verification Steps
docs.text:
1. Confirm Tenant Sharing Settings:
  2. SharePoint Admin Center → Policies → Sharing

  3. EXPECTED: External sharing restricted per FSI requirements

  4. Verify Site-Level Permissions:

  5. Navigate to agent knowledge source site
  6. Site settings → Site permissions

  7. EXPECTED: Only approved groups/users; no "Everyone" groups

  8. Test Agent Access Boundaries:

  9. Ask agent to retrieve content from excluded site

  10. EXPECTED: Agent cannot access or returns "no information found"

  11. Validate Sensitivity Labels:

  12. Check document library → Information panel

  13. EXPECTED: All documents have appropriate sensitivity labels

  14. Confirm External Sharing Blocked:

  15. Attempt to share a document externally from an enterprise-managed site

  16. EXPECTED: Sharing blocked with appropriate message

  17. Test Access Review Notification:

  18. Check that access review notifications are being sent
  19. EXPECTED: Reviewers receive notification per schedule

docs.title: Verification Evidence
docs.text:
• [ ] Screenshot: Tenant-level sharing settings
  • [ ] Export: Site inventory with sharing settings (CSV)
  • [ ] Screenshot: Agent knowledge site permissions
  • [ ] Export: Permission audit for all agent data sources
  • [ ] Screenshot: Sensitivity labels applied to libraries
  • [ ] Documentation: Access review schedule and results

docs.title: Issue: Agent Cannot Access Required Content
docs.text:
Symptoms: Agent returns "I don't have access to that information" for valid content

Solutions:

  1. Verify agent service account is in site members group
  2. Check sensitivity label on content matches agent's allowed labels
  3. Confirm IAG/restricted content discoverability isn't blocking
  4. Review Conditional Access policies that might block service account
  5. Check if site requires MFA and service account can satisfy

docs.title: Issue: Agent Accessing Content It Shouldn't
docs.text:
Symptoms: Agent returning sensitive information from excluded sites

Solutions:

  1. Review agent knowledge source configuration in Copilot Studio
  2. Verify site permissions don't include broad groups
  3. Check for inheritance from parent site
  4. Remove any "Everyone" or "All Users" permissions
  5. Verify sensitivity labels are correctly applied and enforced

docs.title: Issue: Sensitivity Labels Not Appearing on Documents
docs.text:
Symptoms: Documents don't show expected sensitivity labels

Solutions:

  1. Verify labels are published to users in the site
  2. Check if auto-labeling policies are configured
  3. Confirm user has Information Protection client installed
  4. Review if default library label is configured
  5. For existing documents, may need to apply label retroactively

docs.title: Issue: External Sharing Still Possible Despite Settings
docs.text:
Symptoms: Users can share externally from restricted sites

Solutions:

  1. Check site-level override vs. tenant settings (site can't be more permissive)
  2. Verify setting saved correctly (refresh admin center)
  3. Check for PowerShell scripts that may be resetting values
  4. Confirm user isn't a site collection admin (different permissions)
  5. Review SharePoint feature flags for new sharing features

docs.title: Additional Resources
docs.text:
• SharePoint Site Permissions
  • SharePoint Sharing Settings
  • Sensitivity Labels for SharePoint Sites
  • SharePoint Advanced Management
  • SharePoint Admin PowerShell
  • Access Reviews for SharePoint
